name: Seared Skirt Steak with Chipotle and Garlic
recipeIngredient: 3 stemmed, dried chipotle chilies (or canned chipotle chilies)
1 tablespoon olive oil or vegetable oil or rich tasting lard
4 pieces of skirt steak, 5 to 6 ounce each, trimmed of exterior fat
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat broiler.
2. Toast chilies in a heavy skillet over medium heat for about 30 seconds.
3. Rehydrate dried chilies in hot water for about 30 minutes, then drain and discard the soaking liquid.
4. Roast unpeeled garlic on the griddle or skillet, turning occasionally, until soft (they will blacken in spots), about 15 minutes; cool and peel.
5. Roast tomatillos under broiler for 5 minutes, until blackened on one side; flip and repeat on other side.
6. In a food processor, puree tomatillos and their juices, chilies, and three cloves of garlic into a fine textured puree. Taste and season with salt and sugar.
7. Heat the oil or lard in a large heavy skillet over medium heat. When very hot, pat the steak dry, sprinkle with salt, and lay in the pan in a single layer. Sear well on both sides until meat is rare to medium rare, 3 to 4 minutes. Remove to rack set over a plate.
8. Add the onions to the skillet and cook, stirring regularly, until brown but still crunchy, about 5 minutes.
9. Add the salsa to the skillet along with the cumin, pepper, and remaining cloves of garlic. Stir as the salsa reduces for several minutes, then stir in the broth and simmer for about 5 minutes.
10. Return the meat to the pan and let it warm through, about 5 minutes or until the meat is done to your liking. Taste the sauce and add a little more salt and sugar if necessary.
11. With a spatula or slotted spoon, transfer the steaks to a serving platter, then spoon the sauce, onions, and garlic over and around them. Sprinkle with chopped cilantro.
Yield: 4 servings
